figures.caption: Figure 1. Training process for OVD using frozen CLIP. (a) Commonly used training process, (b) Our training process.
Table 1. Comparison with SOTA methods on OV-LVIS (%).
Table 2. Comparison with SOTA methods on OV-COCO (%).

Figure 2. Illustration of the training process of NoOVD. We use K-FPN to extract the pyramid embeddings from frozen CLIP to identify latent novel-category objects. Besides the image-text alignment with Lcons, we also align the features of the RoI head with the features from K-FPN via knowledge self-distillation with Lkd.
Figure 3. Overall of K-FPN (the CLIP Image Encoder is taken as an example with ViT-B/16).
